author: fwyzard
body:
#### PR description:

Preliminary work:

  - Move `QueueCache` and `EventCache` to `HeterogeneousCore/AlpakaInterface`
    `QueueCache` and `EventCache` are the only part of `HeterogeneousCore/AlpakaCore` safe to use in a `.dev.cc` file, so it makes sense for them to be under `HeterogeneousCore/AlpakaInterface` instead.
    Eventually the same functionality should be upstreamed  to alpaka (v2 or v3).

  - Simplify the interface of `EDMetadata`
    Change the interface of the constructor for the asynchronous backends to match the synchronous backend.
    Instead of taking an alpaka event as an argument to the constructor, allocate one only when needed.
    Tested with `CMSSW_16_1_0_pre4` running the Run 3 HLT menu `/online/collisions/2026/2e34/v1.1/HLT/V2` over 10k events from run 402360, on a machine with 2 AMD "Bergamo" 9754 128 cores and 4 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, with 16 jobs with 32 threads and 24 streams, shows no impact on performance:
      - before: 1081.1  1.7 ev/s
      - after:  1081.8  1.8 ev/s    

  - Add an `EDMetadataSentry` constructor with an explicit queue argument


Implement a new kind of alpaka `stream::EDProducer` with a fixed association of device queues (_e.g._ CUDA streams) to framework streams.

This is useful for using external software that associates resources to the device queues, for example the PyTorch device memory caching allocator.

Migrating the PyTorch alpaka modules from `stream::EDProducer` to `stream::FixedQueueEDProducer` ensures that PyTorch sees only a limited number of device queues, reducing the overall device memory utilisation.

For more background information see the presentation [ML inference on GPUs in CMSSW with PyTorch](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1667896/#6-ml-inference-on-gpus-in-cmss) by @EmanueleCoradin at the [CMS developments with GPUs](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1667896/) on March 30th, 2026.


#### PR validation:

All unit tests pass.
title: Implement `stream::FixedQueueEDProducer`
